Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Succesor Trustee; And Denial Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Continued To Oct-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for Kathryn Guillen (Petitioner).
Mr. Dixon (Real Estate Agent) also present.

The Court noted that Mr. Del Prado is not present. The Court also noted that the petition request that Mr. Del Prado be removed as a Trustee and that Ms. Guillen be appointed as the sole Trustee, that under the terms of the trust if the two Trustees do not agree the remedy is to appoint a third Trustee and that the provision is that if a Trustee is unwilling to act, then a Trustee could act alone. In addition, the Court stated that it appears Mr. Del Prado just doesn't like what is going on and questions whether the request should be to appoint a third Trustee. Mr. Jew responded yes to requesting a third trustee, stated that Mr. Del Prado is slowly moving forward, and that Mr. Del Prado finally signed a listing agreement and has accepted an offer for purchase with a closing date of September 20, 2019 and a vacate date of September 18, 2019. Mr. Jew also stated that arrangements have been made to help Mr. Del Prado move, that Mr. Del Prado has kind of disappeared, that he has done this before and came back, that they are hoping he will pop up again and that maybe this won't be needed. The Petition to Remove Trustee and Appoint a Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to October 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Succesor Trustee; And Denial Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Continued To Sep-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for Kathryn Guillen (Petitioner).
Rene Del Prado in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it received the peremptory challenge, that it reviewed it, that it is striking the peremptory challenge as it does not comply with CCP 170.6, that a Party can only challenge a Judge before whom there is an issue pending or has been assigned for trial, and that Judge Busch does not have a matter before him or a trial before him. Judge Quinn also stated that he believes the challenge was done to Judge Busch in error and that it was meant for him, and advised Mr. Del Prado that he has a right to do an oral challenge if he chooses. Mr. Del Prado is sworn in and under oath presents an oral motion to disqualify Judge Quinn from presiding over the matter. The Court notes that it is a valid peremptory challenge. The Petition to Remove Trustee and to Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard by another Judicial Officer in the Probate Department.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Succesor Trustee; And Denial Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Aug-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for and with Kathryn Guillen (Petitioner).
Guillermo Guillen (Husband of Kathryn) also present.
Rene Del Prado in Propria Persona.

Mr. Jew stated that Mr. Del Prado agreed to sign a listing agreement 60-days ago, that there is the pressing issue of a reverse mortgage in the amount of $950,000.00, that one extension was granted, that a second extension has been granted up to October, and that the second extension informed them that this will be the last extension and that if nothing happens, they will proceed with foreclosure. Mr. Jew also stated that an initial date for viewing of Mr. Del Prado's unit was scheduled, that Mr. Del Prado cancelled it, that there is a Broker's tour tomorrow, that he is wondering if that's going to go through and that the concern of his Client is to get this to where it needs to be. Mr. Del Prado stated that he was not aware of the granting of the second extension, that he was not notified by the API Attorney, that they (lender) will not talk to Ms. Fan, that they still have the last Attorney's name and that he is cooperating with the sale. The Court indicated to Mr. Del Prado that making the property unavailable for viewing is not cooperating, noted that he was aware of the first extension and hasn't taken proper action, stated that at the forefront is the Parents wishes as stated in the trust for the property to be distributed upon the death of the surviving Spouse, and that because of no cooperation from the Trustees, maybe the route to go is having a third Trustee. Mr. Del Prado stated that one of the hold ups is that there are a lot of problems with the property, that it needs a new roof and that he has not seen an a copy of the inspection report. The Court informed Mr. Del Prado that the reverse mortgage is a big deal, that there is some pressure now and that the petition has been pending for some time now. Mr. Jew stated that they have received a couple of verbal offers in the ball park of $1,400,000.00 and requested a short continuance. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. is continued to August 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to talk about how a third Trustee comes in.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Succesor Trustee; And Denial Of Trustee Fees
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for and with Kathryn Guillen (Petitioner).
Guillermo Guillen (Kathryn's Husband) also present.

Mr. Jew stated that Mr. Del Prado has been cooperating as far as the sale, that the deadline for the sale is currently at July 4th, that they have asked for an extension and that they have not heard back. Mr. Jew also stated that Mr. Del Prado executed a listing agreement, that he has gone a couple of steps forward and then stopped, and that there is some concern that they may get stuck and he will not see this through. In addition, Mr. Jew stated that Mr. Del Prado resides in the basement unit, that there is concern that there may an issue when he has to move, that they are still asking for removal, and that rather than having the Court issue a ruling, they are requesting a 30-day continuance since he is cooperating. The Petition to Remove Trustee and to Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to July 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
